---
title: "Credit Risk Early-Warning System (Single-Quarter Version)"
author: "Your Name"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
source("R/00_packages.R")
source("R/01_config.R")

feature_panel <- readRDS(file.path(dir_features, "feature_panel.rds"))
watchlist <- readr::read_csv(file.path(dir_watchlists, "watchlist.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
metrics <- readr::read_csv(file.path(dir_metrics, "logistic_metrics.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
```

# Executive Summary

This project builds a **credit risk scoring model** using publicly available U.S. banking data.

Because the dataset contains a single reporting period, the analysis focuses on **cross-sectional risk classification** rather than time-based prediction.

The model identifies banks with relatively higher credit risk and produces a ranked **watchlist** to support risk monitoring.

# Business Objective

The main question is:

> Which banks currently look higher risk based on their financial ratios?

# Data Overview

```{r}
glimpse(feature_panel)
```

# Target Definition

The target variable `high_risk` is defined as:

- banks in the top 25% of net charge-off ratios

```{r}
feature_panel %>% count(high_risk)
```

# Model Performance

```{r}
metrics
```

# Top 20 Watchlist

```{r}
watchlist %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  select(bank_name, pred_prob, risk_bucket, roa, npa_ratio)
```

# Key Visualizations

## Top 20 Risky Banks

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(dir_figures, "top_20_risky_banks.png"))
```

## Risk Distribution

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(file.path(dir_figures, "risk_bucket_distribution.png"))
```

# Conclusion

This project demonstrates:
- working with real financial data
- feature engineering for credit risk
- building and evaluating models
- translating predictions into a business-style watchlist
